title: Offline queue flushes when connectivity returns
steps:
You are signed in on the app's home screen (see 'Before you start'). Airplane mode will be used in this scenario — disable it at the end.

1. On the device, enable Airplane mode via Settings or the Control Center / Quick Settings shortcut. Confirm the status bar shows offline (airplane icon or no wifi/cellular indicator).

2. Return to the app. Navigate to Compose (tap the + button at the bottom-right).

3. Create a post with title 'UAT-offline-<your-tester-name>' and body 'queued offline'. Tap Submit.

4. Observe the app behavior. Note what the Submit button does, what message appears, and whether the Outbox / Drafts / Queued section shows the item.

5. Disable Airplane mode. Wait up to 15 seconds for connectivity to fully return (status bar icon confirms).

6. Return to the app if you switched away. Observe any sync indicator.

7. Navigate to the Posts list (Home tab) and pull to refresh.

8. Cleanup: long-press your 'UAT-offline-<your-tester-name>' post, tap Delete, confirm the prompt. Confirm the post disappears from the list.
expected:
Step 4: Submit button remains enabled OR shows a 'queued' state. A visible banner, toast, or Outbox badge indicates the post is queued for sync. No error dialog appears. App does not crash.

Step 6: A sync indicator (spinner, toast, or banner) appears briefly as the queue drains. The indicator disappears when sync completes.

Step 7: Your 'UAT-offline-<your-tester-name>' post is visible in the Posts list with a normal (non-queued) state indicator.

Step 8: After deletion, the post no longer appears in the Posts list.

PASS if all four observations confirm: queued behavior on submit, sync indicator on reconnect, post visible after refresh, cleanup removes it. FAIL if the app crashed, the post was lost without warning on offline submit, or the post never synced after reconnect (ask the Orchestrator to purge it manually if the cleanup step can't reach it).
